class.name: OcctlGraphHandle
class.brief_description: Handle wrapper for occtl_graph_t with built-in meshing methods.
class.description:
Auto-generated handle for the opaque C type [code]occtl_graph_t[/code]. Wraps a C pointer, auto-frees it on destruction, and provides convenient meshing methods ([method mesh_faces], [method mesh_edges], [method mesh_vertices]) that are attached directly on the handle.
All meshing methods accept an optional [code]existing[/code] parameter to reuse a previously created ArrayMesh/MultiMesh — this preserves any user customizations (e.g. shader materials) while replacing the geometry.

method.name: set_handle
method.description: Set the wrapped C pointer from an int64_t handle.

method.name: get_handle
method.description: Get the wrapped C pointer as an int64_t handle.

method.name: is_valid
method.description: Check if the handle wraps a non-null pointer.

method.name: release
method.description: Release ownership of the C pointer and return the handle. The destructor will not free it.

method.name: free
method.description: Manually free the wrapped C pointer.

method.name: mesh_faces
method.description:
Triangulate faces into an [ArrayMesh].
- [code]face_ids[/code]: [code]null[/code] for all faces, or a [PackedInt64Array] of face node IDs. - [code]include_normals[/code]: Compute angle-thresholded smooth normals (sharp/smooth edges are handled automatically based on the angle limit in [code]options[/code]). - [code]include_uvs[/code]: Include UV coordinates if available. - [code]include_tangents[/code]: Compute tangent vectors (requires normals and UVs). - [code]include_feature_ids[/code]: Encode face node IDs as vertex colors (one colour per face). - [code]options[/code]: Meshing options controlling deflection, angle, etc. When [code]null[/code], defaults are used. - [code]existing[/code]: Optional [ArrayMesh] to reuse. Surfaces are cleared and re-created, but user customizations (materials, shaders) are preserved.
The cached triangulation is invalidated when deflection or angle parameters change.

method.name: mesh_edges
method.description:
Build edge tube instances as a [MultiMesh].
A low-resolution cylinder mesh is instanced along each edge polyline segment using [MultiMesh] transforms — one cylinder per segment — for efficient rendering.
- [code]edge_ids[/code]: [code]null[/code] for all edges, or a [PackedInt64Array] of edge node IDs. - [code]radius[/code]: Tube radius. Default [code]0.01[/code]. If [code]<= 0[/code], uses [code]options.deflection * 10[/code]. The radius is automatically scaled by the graph's bounding box diagonal for consistent visual size across different models. - [code]options[/code]: Meshing options (controls cylinder resolution via the angle parameter). - [code]existing[/code]: Optional [MultiMesh] to reuse (mesh and transforms are replaced, user customizations preserved).

method.name: mesh_vertices
method.description:
Build vertex sphere instances as a [MultiMesh].
A low-resolution sphere mesh is instanced at each vertex position using [MultiMesh] transforms for efficient rendering.
- [code]vertex_ids[/code]: [code]null[/code] for all vertices, or a [PackedInt64Array] of vertex node IDs. - [code]radius[/code]: Sphere radius. Default [code]0.02[/code] (double the edge radius default). If [code]<= 0[/code], uses [code]options.deflection * 10[/code]. The radius is automatically scaled by the graph's bounding box diagonal. - [code]options[/code]: Meshing options (controls sphere resolution via the angle parameter). - [code]existing[/code]: Optional [MultiMesh] to reuse.
